entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: An important pulse crop grown and consumed globally, especially in Afro-Asian countries, known for its nutritional quality and health benefits.

entities.name: AFRO-ASIAN COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions where chickpea is predominantly grown and consumed.

entities.name: CARBOHYDRATES
entities.description: Nutritional component of chickpea, including starch and sugars.

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: High-quality nutritional component of chickpea containing essential amino acids.

entities.name: ESSENTIAL AMINO ACIDS
entities.description: Amino acids required by the body found in chickpea protein.

entities.name: SULPHUR-CONTAINING AMINO ACIDS
entities.description: Amino acids that are lacking in chickpea and can be complemented by cereals.

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Crops that can be added to the diet to complement the amino acid profile of chickpea.

entities.name: STARCH
entities.description: The major storage carbohydrate present in chickpea.

entities.name: DIETARY FIBRE
entities.description: A carbohydrate component found in chickpea.

entities.name: OLIGOSACCHARIDES
entities.description: A carbohydrate component found in chickpea.

entities.name: SIMPLE SUGARS
entities.description: Carbohydrates in chickpea, specifically glucose and sucrose.

entities.name: GLUCOSE
entities.description: A type of simple sugar found in chickpea.

entities.name: SUCROSE
entities.description: A type of simple sugar found in chickpea.

entities.name: LIPIDS
entities.description: Fats present in low amounts in chickpea.

entities.name: UNSATURATED FATTY ACIDS
entities.description: Nutritionally important fats found in chickpea, such as linoleic and oleic acids.

entities.name: LINOLEIC ACIDS
entities.description: A type of unsaturated fatty acid present in chickpea.

entities.name: OLEIC ACIDS
entities.description: A type of unsaturated fatty acid present in chickpea.

entities.name: B-SITOSTEROL
entities.description: A sterol present in chickpea oil.

entities.name: CAMPEROSTEROL
entities.description: A sterol present in chickpea oil.

entities.name: STIGMASTEROL
entities.description: A sterol present in chickpea oil.

entities.name: CALCIUM
entities.description: A mineral present in chickpea seeds.

entities.name: MAGNESIUM
entities.description: A mineral present in chickpea seeds.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A mineral present in chickpea seeds.

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A mineral present in chickpea seeds.

entities.name: VITAMINS
entities.description: Important micronutrients found in chickpea, including riboflavin, niacin, thiamin, folate, and b-carotene.

entities.name: RIBOFLAVIN
entities.description: A vitamin found in chickpea.

entities.name: NIACIN
entities.description: A vitamin found in chickpea.

entities.name: THIAMIN
entities.description: A vitamin found in chickpea.

entities.name: FOLATE
entities.description: A vitamin found in chickpea.

entities.name: B-CAROTENE
entities.description: A vitamin A precursor found in chickpea.

entities.name: ANTI-NUTRITIONAL FACTORS
entities.description: Compounds in chickpea seeds that can be reduced or eliminated by cooking.

entities.name: COOKING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Processes used to reduce or eliminate anti-nutritional factors in chickpea.

entities.name: CVD
entities.description: Cardiovascular disease that may be positively affected by chickpea consumption.

entities.name: TYPE 2 DIABETES
entities.description: A metabolic disease that may be positively affected by chickpea consumption.

entities.name: DIGESTIVE DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases of the digestive system that may be positively affected by chickpea consumption.

entities.name: CANCERS
entities.description: Diseases that may be positively affected by chickpea consumption.
